Question: If $$a = 3 + 4 i$$ , what is the product $$a^{*} a$$ ?

Suppose that a particle with energy E is moving along the x -axis and is confined in the region between 0 and L . One possible wave function is $$\psi ( x , t ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}A e^{\text{unicode[:-]} i E t \text{/} \hslash} \text{sin} \frac{\pi x}{L} , & \text{when} 0 \le x \le L \\ 0 , & \text{otherwise}\end{matrix}\right . .$$ Determine the normalization constant.
For the particle in the above example, find the probability of locating it between positions 0 and L /4
